# Instagram Post — OpenAI Partners with Accenture, BCG, McKinsey, and Capgemini to Scale Agentic AI in the Enterprise

---

OpenAI just locked in the enterprise distribution channel.

On February 23rd, OpenAI announced "Frontier Alliances" — formal partnerships with Accenture, Boston Consulting Group, Capgemini, and McKinsey. Their mission: drive enterprise adoption of Frontier, OpenAI's agentic AI platform for building and orchestrating AI-powered agents across business operations.

This is a go-to-market play, not a product launch. These four firms collectively influence the technology decisions of most of the world's largest companies. When they walk into a boardroom recommending an agentic platform, that carries weight no benchmark can match.

The pattern echoes the cloud era — where consulting partnerships determined which platforms became enterprise defaults. But agentic AI is stickier. Once agents are embedded in workflows, switching costs escalate fast.

For executives, the critical move right now is evaluating agentic platforms with independence before consulting-driven momentum narrows the options.

GYanTech AI provides vendor-independent agentic AI strategy for leadership teams navigating exactly this moment. Link in bio.

## Fact-check notes
- OpenAI announced 'Frontier Alliances' partnerships with Accenture, Boston Consulting Group, Capgemini, and McKinsey on February 23, 2026 (Source: SiliconAngle)
- The partnerships are designed to help enterprise customers adopt OpenAI's Frontier platform for building, deploying, and orchestrating AI-powered agents (Source: SiliconAngle)
- Frontier is OpenAI's recently launched agentic AI platform for building, deploying, and orchestrating AI-powered co-workers (Source: SiliconAngle)
